report_name: UnitedHealth Confirms Paying Ransom in Crippling Change Healthcare Attack

threat_actors.name: BlackCat/ALPHV
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) operation responsible for the February 2024 attack on Change Healthcare. The group has links to former members of DarkSide and BlackMatter and uses double or triple extortion tactics.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware deployment and data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Initial access was gained via a compromised Citrix remote access portal that lacked multi-factor authentication.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used stolen credentials to log into the compromised Citrix remote access service.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors exfiltrated 6 terabytes of sensitive patient data before deploying the ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: BlackCat ransomware was deployed to encrypt systems at Change Healthcare, causing massive disruptions to the U.S. healthcare system.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used remote services to move laterally within the Change Healthcare network after gaining initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The BlackCat ransomware attempts to delete volume shadow copies using wmic.exe to prevent system recovery.

malware.name: BlackCat
malware.description: The ransomware payload deployed by the BlackCat/ALPHV group to encrypt systems at Change Healthcare and exfiltrate 6 terabytes of data.

tools.name: rundll32.exe
tools.description: BlackCat ransomware often uses rundll32.exe to execute its payload.

tools.name: wmic.exe
tools.description: Used by BlackCat ransomware to delete volume shadow copies and inhibit system recovery.

identities.name: UnitedHealth Group
identities.description: Parent company of Change Healthcare that confirmed paying a $22 million ransom following the ransomware attack. The company reported an $872 million cost in Q1 2024 due to the incident.

identities.name: Change Healthcare
identities.description: A subsidiary of UnitedHealth Group and a critical part of the U.S. healthcare infrastructure that was crippled by a BlackCat/ALPHV ransomware attack in February 2024.

identities.name: Citrix
identities.description: Vendor of the remote access portal that was compromised for initial access due to a lack of multi-factor authentication.

infrastructure.name: Compromised Citrix Portal
infrastructure.description: A Citrix remote access portal that lacked multi-factor authentication was used as the initial access vector for the attack on Change Healthcare.

indicators.name: BlackCat Payload Execution via Rundll32
indicators.pattern:
title: BlackCat Payload Execution via Rundll32
id: 7e8b1c2a-3d4e-4f5a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of rundll32.exe, which is a common method for BlackCat to execute its payload.
references:
  - https://www.securityandcompliance.com/news/major-cyber-attacks-data-breaches-ransomware-attacks-in-april-2024
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-27
tags:
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of rundll32.exe. Requires correlation with other suspicious activity.
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of rundll32.exe, a technique noted in hunting hints for BlackCat activity.

indicators.name: BlackCat Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
indicators.pattern:
title: BlackCat Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
id: f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects attempts to delete volume shadow copies using wmic.exe, a common ransomware behavior to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.securityandcompliance.com/news/major-cyber-attacks-data-breaches-ransomware-attacks-in-april-2024
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-27
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, though rare.
level: high
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of wmic.exe to delete shadow copies, a TTP used by BlackCat to prevent recovery.

relationships.source_name: BlackCat Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
BlackCat Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
